title: Idempotency, Inbox Transactions, and Effects · Program Defense
summary: A release board compares near-miss Go and RabbitMQ decisions for idempotency, inbox transactions, and effects and accepts only bounded ownership, causal evidence, and an explicit live-broker transfer gate. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a duplicate-safe Go effect state machine. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Derive a stable scoped operation identity from producer intent, tenant, message type, and semantic version rather than delivery metadata; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

Record inbox identity, input fingerprint, state transition, domain effect, and completion atomically in one database transaction where possible; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Reject reuse of an operation identity with a different payload fingerprint or semantic intent and retain conflict evidence; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Stable Idempotency Key · changed case · Quiz Stable Idempotency Key
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz Go messaging team handles case 4503 during consumer shutdown drill with one blocked handler goroutine. Build deterministic Go evidence to reject fingerprint conflicts, suppress completed duplicates, and preserve external ambiguity; retain rollback topology before deleting old bindings; then exhaust retry age before attempts reach their maximum. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Derive a stable scoped operation identity from producer intent, tenant, message type, and semantic version rather than delivery metadata; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

For diagnose the hidden failure, run one convenient Inbox Retention Replay happy path, omit ownership, error, changed input, goroutine stop, and full-toolchain checks, then accept plausible output.
Run the boundary case, inspect the actual mechanism, then accept or repair the claim.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Inbox Retention Replay
steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Inbox Retention Replay: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A quiz Go messaging team handles case 9028 during race-enabled integration test with duplicate delivery. Build deterministic Go evidence to reject fingerprint conflicts, suppress completed duplicates, and preserve external ambiguity; retain rollback topology before deleting old bindings; then cancel the parent context while a result is still ambiguous. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Set inbox retention from maximum redelivery, replay, audit, privacy, and disaster-recovery windows and test expired-key behavior; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Inbox Retention Replay.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For RabbitMQ and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Inbox Retention Replay, keep Inbox Retention Replay connected to executable Go messaging decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Set inbox retention from maximum redelivery, replay, audit, privacy, and disaster-recovery windows and test expired-key behavior; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

Build deterministic Go evidence to reject fingerprint conflicts, suppress completed duplicates, and preserve external ambiguity; retain rollback topology before deleting old bindings; then cancel the parent context while a result is still ambiguous. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Set inbox retention from maximum redelivery, replay, audit, privacy, and disaster-recovery windows and test expired-key behavior; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

For RabbitMQ and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Inbox Retention Replay, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Inbox records can be deleted immediately after acknowledgement because RabbitMQ will not redeliver. In Go, a sync.Map of seen IDs supplies durable cross-process idempotency.
